description_long: This object consists of several interlocking concrete jersey barriers, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. The barriers are rectangular with a sloped top edge and vertical grooves along the face, providing both structural strength and visual distinction. Some segments are straight, while others are angled or end pieces, allowing for flexible arrangement in various configurations. The surface is a rough, matte gray concrete, showing subtle textural variations typical of cast concrete. The barriers are solid and heavy, designed to resist vehicle impact and prevent unauthorized access. No reflective strips, graffiti, or warning signs are visible, and the barriers appear to be in good condition with only minor scuffs.
description: A group of solid, gray concrete jersey barriers with sloped tops and vertical grooves, arranged for urban traffic control. The surfaces are rough and matte, showing minor scuffs but no markings or signs.
description_view_0: This view shows the front faces of two main jersey barriers with vertical grooves, as well as angled and end pieces positioned nearby.
description_view_1: This view highlights the long, continuous front faces of the barriers, emphasizing their length and the alignment of the grooves.
description_view_2: This view displays the rear and side profiles, revealing the thickness and blocky end pieces of the barriers.
description_view_3: This view shows the back sides of the barriers, with the angled and end pieces more clearly separated from the main segments.
